creator: trix@basement.net
text:
Created attachment 189527
redefine dhcpd_rootdir to dhcpd_chrootdir for chroot-ed service

Using syslog in a chrooted DHCPd (still) does not work.  The syslog script does not pick up the chroot directory, therefore it does not create the syslog socket and no logging is available.

I have a new patch that enables the function.  I believe the rest of the PR may still apply with minor changes.

Net effect being that the rc.conf variable "dhcpd_rootdir" becomes "dhcpd_chrootdir" so that it can be picked up by the /etc/rc.d/syslogd script the same way as ntpd and named.
